content: Cantaloupe has a glycemic index of 60 and is exceptionally high in vitamin K, aiding vitamin D absorption.
Cantaloupe seeds contain omega-3 fatty acids, similar to those found in fish oil.
Avocados have a glycemic index of 14 and are rich in healthy fats, slowing the breakdown of carbohydrates.
Grapefruits have a glycemic index of 25 and are beneficial for lowering blood lipid levels and cholesterol.
Plums have a glycemic index of 22 and are low in sugar due to their slow digestion and high fiber content.
Strawberries have a glycemic load of less than 3 per 100 grams, making them a low glycemic fruit with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits.
